evals.prompt: I need to make this dashboard work well on iPhone. Right now it's desktop-only and our mobile users are complaining they can't use it.
evals.expected_output: A mobile-adapted dashboard with bottom tab navigation (3-5 items), stats cards reflowed for small screens, transactions table converted to a card/list layout, chart with a text headline/summary, safe-area padding, and dvh viewport units.
assertions: Navigation is converted to a bottom-positioned tab bar with 5 or fewer primary items, not a hamburger menu or vertically stacked sidebar links
Output includes env(safe-area-inset-bottom) padding on the bottom navigation bar
Output uses dvh (100dvh or min-h-[100dvh]) instead of 100vh for the main layout container
The desktop table is preserved in the DOM (e.g., hidden md:table) alongside the mobile card list, so screen-reader users retain row/column association
The chart section includes a headline number or text summary above or alongside the chart visualization
Interactive elements (nav tabs, buttons, list items) have a minimum touch target size of 44px in at least one dimension

evals.prompt: this checkout form is totally broken on mobile — the keyboard covers the submit button and the two-column layout is cramped on my phone. can you fix it?
evals.expected_output: A single-column form layout with inputmode='decimal' for currency/numeric fields, enterkeyhint attributes for keyboard flow, and a submit button that remains visible when the keyboard is open.
assertions: Input fields use text-base (16px) or larger font size to prevent iOS Safari auto-zoom on focus — not text-sm (14px) which triggers automatic viewport zoom
Currency or numeric inputs (Tip Amount, CVV, ZIP) use inputMode='decimal' or inputMode='numeric' instead of type='number'
At least one input includes an enterKeyHint attribute ('next', 'done', or 'send')
The submit button is NOT position:fixed at the bottom — it flows inline with the form, or uses a keyboard-safe sticky pattern with safe-area padding
The form container uses dvh (min-h-[100dvh]) instead of vh
Submit footer includes safe-area padding via env(safe-area-inset-bottom) or a max() fallback for the home indicator

evals.prompt: adapt this customer analytics page for phones
evals.expected_output: Table converted to a card/list layout on mobile with always-visible action buttons, adequate tap targets, and toolbar controls sized for touch.
assertions: Search input uses text-base (16px) on mobile to prevent iOS Safari auto-zoom — not text-sm which triggers automatic viewport zoom on focus
Row actions (view, edit, delete) are always visible on mobile — the opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 pattern is removed or overridden for small screens
Action buttons are at least 44x44 CSS px on mobile — the p-1 with w-3.5 h-3.5 icons (28px total) is enlarged
Toolbar icons (filter, download) are enlarged to at least 44x44 CSS px tap targets — the p-1.5 with w-4 h-4 (30px) originals are not adequate
The mobile layout does not cause horizontal document-level scroll

evals.prompt: our product page hover effects don't work on mobile and the grid is too cramped. customers on phones are having trouble adding items to cart.
evals.expected_output: Product grid reflowed to 1-2 columns on mobile, hover overlay replaced with always-visible touch controls, filter sidebar converted to a sheet/drawer, and action buttons sized for touch.
assertions: 'Add to Cart' and 'Quick View' actions are visible without hover on mobile — the opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 overlay is replaced with always-visible controls
Product grid uses 1-2 columns on mobile instead of grid-cols-4
The sidebar filter panel is converted to a drawer, sheet, modal, or collapsible section on mobile — not rendered as a permanent 240px sidebar
The wishlist heart button is always visible on mobile and meets 44px minimum tap target size
Checkbox tap targets in the filter section are at least 44px (the w-3.5 h-3.5 originals are 14px)

evals.prompt: audit our app layout for mobile web — we haven't done any mobile optimization yet and we're launching mobile support next week. here's our main layout component.
evals.expected_output: A prioritized audit identifying missing viewport-fit=cover, vh→dvh, hamburger→bottom tab bar, missing safe-area padding, with highest-risk issues reported first and concrete code changes.
assertions: Output identifies and recommends adding viewport-fit=cover to the viewport meta tag
Output identifies and recommends replacing h-[100vh] with h-[100dvh] or min-h-[100dvh]
Output recommends converting the hamburger menu to a bottom tab bar with the 5 primary nav items
Output adds env(safe-area-inset-bottom) padding to bottom-positioned elements
Output presents the highest-risk issues first before listing secondary changes
Output keeps landscape phones in the mobile shell — does not introduce a sidebar or split-view for phone-width viewports rotated to landscape
